topic: Sharp phase-transition theorem for Honest-DiD sensitivity analysis (Rambachan and Roth, 2023, Review of Economic Studies) and its descendants (Roth 2024 NBER). Honest-DiD relaxes the parallel trends assumption by bounding the deviation of the treatment-cohort post-trend from a linear pre-trend extrapolation by an M-budget, and reports a robust confidence interval CI(M) for the dynamic ATT that grows monotonically in M.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a sharp closed-form formula for the Honest-DiD breakdown threshold at which CI(M) first contains zero in staggered event-study designs.

source_refs.claim: Rambachan and Roth recommend reporting robust confidence sets over M and show empirical breakdown values, but compute them through the Honest-DiD sensitivity procedure rather than a symbolic threshold formula.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth

source_refs.excerpt: partial identification occurs precisely when this set is a nonempty proper subset
sharply separates the point-identified, partially-identified, and non-identified regimes

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published Honest-DiD method is formulated as robust inference over polyhedral restrictions and test inversion at fixed M, while the prior ThmSmith spectral-phase proposal left the singular-value asymptotics, boundary constants, and growth restrictions unresolved. The combined gap is the missing observable closed-form threshold that turns the sensitivity curve into a theorem-level phase transition rather than a computed plot.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem giving M_star as an explicit functional of the event-study point estimates, covariance matrix, ATT loading, and smoothness operator, with proof that it is the sharp first zero-crossing of CI(M).

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the active-face phase diagram of Honest-DiD confidence sets as M varies, including the exact zero-excluded, tangent, and zero-included regimes.

source_refs.claim: Their conditional confidence-set construction reduces the null to linear inequalities under polyhedral restrictions, but does not classify which constraint face controls CI(M) across M.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth

source_refs.claim: Linear conditional moment-inequality inference delivers validity with nuisance parameters, leaving symbolic active-set geometry as a separate problem.
source_refs.author: Isaiah Andrews, Jonathan Roth, and Ariel Pakes

source_refs.excerpt: routine linear algebra but is not yet formalized in this exact row-space/box form
add a concrete finite-dimensional lemma for affine fibers

open_problems.why_unsolved: The obstacle is not the existence of a valid confidence procedure, but the absence of a finite-dimensional active-face theorem that names the controlling dual vertex, slope, and tie cases as M crosses critical values. Prior ThmSmith work separately identified the missing row-space/box affine-fiber lemma needed to make this geometry reusable.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A polyhedral active-set theorem giving M-intervals, endpoint formulas, and singular or tie cases for each face of the Honest-DiD feasible set.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify rank or singular-value thresholds of the staggered cohort-period aggregation map that determine when aggregate Honest-DiD sensitivity is sharp, conservative, or misleading.

source_refs.claim: Group-time ATT parameters are identified under staggered timing and conditional parallel trends, but sensitivity thresholds for violations are not derived at the aggregation-operator level.
source_refs.author: Brantly Callaway and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Staggered TWFE lead and lag coefficients can be contaminated by treatment effects from other periods, so apparent pretrends need not equal untreated-trend violations.
source_refs.author: Liyang Sun and Sarah Abraham

source_refs.claim: Stacked DiD can put different implicit weights on treatment and control trends, so aggregation can change the target and the robustness interpretation.
source_refs.author: Coady Wing, Seth M. Freedman, and Alex Hollingsworth

source_refs.excerpt: Sharp partial identification of staggered difference-in-differences under partially unobserved pre-trends

open_problems.why_unsolved: Modern staggered-DiD papers diagnose contamination and define better group-time estimands, while prior ThmSmith work flags partially observed pretrend summaries as a sharpness problem. What is missing is the operator-level condition saying when the pretrend and posttrend information surviving aggregation still supports a sharp Honest-DiD budget.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem relating rank, null spaces, and smallest singular values of the cohort-to-event aggregation map to sharp identified sets and breakdown thresholds for aggregate versus cohort-level Honest-DiD analysis.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend bounded-anticipation staggered-DiD sharp intervals beyond variation-independent anticipation boxes to cross-cohort or cross-period coupled restrictions.

source_refs.excerpt: without that assumption the same displayed interval is only an outer bound
if substantive restrictions couple anticipation distortions...the support interval may be conservative

open_problems.why_unsolved: The banked DiD anticipation result obtains sharpness only because each anticipation coordinate can vary independently in a box. Honest-DiD smoothness restrictions are typically coupled across event time, so the same displayed support interval becomes only an outer bound unless the coupled support function is solved.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp support-function formula or LP dual for coupled anticipation and trend-deviation budgets, with examples showing exactly when the independent-box interval is conservative.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop uniformly valid inference for the estimated Honest-DiD breakdown point or breakdown frontier itself, rather than for CI(M) at fixed M.

source_refs.claim: The paper reports sensitivity plots and breakdown values, but its formal coverage guarantees are stated for robust confidence sets under specified restrictions.
source_refs.author: Ashesh Rambachan and Jonathan Roth

source_refs.claim: Breakdown frontiers quantify how much baseline assumptions can be relaxed before a conclusion changes, creating a target distinct from a fixed-assumption confidence set.
source_refs.author: Matthew A. Masten and Alexandre Poirier

open_problems.why_unsolved: The breakdown point is a minimum over a set-valued inference procedure indexed by M. Fixed-M uniform coverage does not by itself provide distribution theory or simultaneous coverage for the random crossing time, especially at kinks or weakly binding constraints.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A confidence interval or band for M_star with uniform coverage, local asymptotic theory at kinks, and analytic or bootstrap critical values valid under weakly and strongly binding constraints.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine when Honest-DiD robustness thresholds are invariant to outcome transformations or continuous-treatment scaling, and when M_star is a functional-form artifact.

source_refs.claim: Parallel trends is insensitive to functional form only under strong restrictions, so robustness conclusions can depend on the outcome scale.
source_refs.author: Jonathan Roth and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: For continuous treatments, interpreting differences across treatment doses is challenging because generalized parallel trends does not rule out selection bias across doses.
source_refs.author: Brantly Callaway, Andrew Goodman-Bacon, and Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna

open_problems.why_unsolved: M_star is computed in the scale of event-study coefficients, and transformations can change pretrend curvature, covariance, and post-period effect magnitude. Existing functional-form and continuous-treatment results show the risk but do not give a sharp invariance theorem for Honest-DiD thresholds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Necessary and sufficient invariance conditions, plus sharp counterexamples and transformation-adjusted formulas for M_star under level, log, and continuous-dose event-study specifications.

literature_map:
Rambachan and Roth (2023, REStud) provide the central Honest-DiD partial-ID framework, bounding post-trend violations by pretrend-based restrictions and reporting CI(M).
Roth (2022, AER Insights) shows why binary pretrend testing is unreliable: low power and post-pretest distortions motivate continuous sensitivity thresholds.
Manski and Pepper (2018, REStat) and Masten and Poirier (2020, QE) are foundational partial-ID anchors for bounded-variation assumptions and breakdown frontiers.
Andrews, Roth, and Pakes (2023, REStud/NBER) supply linear conditional moment-inequality machinery behind valid inference with nuisance parameters.
Kwon and Roth (2024, AEA P&P) shift from worst-case bounds toward Bayes and empirical-Bayes priors learned from pretrends, creating a tension between minimax M and posterior calibration.
Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, JoE) identify group-time ATT under staggered timing and conditional parallel trends, but do not solve sensitivity thresholds for violations.
Goodman-Bacon (2021, JoE), de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER), and Sun and Abraham (2021, JoE) expose weighting, negative-weight, and contamination problems in TWFE event studies.
Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, REStud) provide robust imputation event-study estimation while largely taking identifying restrictions as given.
Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, JPE Micro) optimize estimation for staggered rollouts, opening covariance-aware threshold questions for efficient estimators.
Wing, Freedman, and Hollingsworth (2024, NBER) show stacked DiD can use different implicit treatment/control trend weights, linking sensitivity to composition balance.
Recent staggered-adoption work on cohort anchoring and aggregation warns that aggregate robust inference can be misleading when pre and post coefficients pool different cohorts.
Roth and Sant'Anna (2023, Econometrica) and Callaway, Goodman-Bacon, and Sant'Anna (2024, NBER) show functional-form and continuous-treatment choices can change what parallel trends means, so M_star may not be scale-invariant.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected active state for panel_honest_did_phase/v1; no proposal tex existed yet.
Highest-yield panel source was q1_spectral_phase_transition/p1_markov: final proposal, angle0_rejected tex, README, and review log.
Most directly DiD-related source was pid_did_anticipation_bounded/v1: proposal, Stage 0 derivation, README, angle reviews, and Stage 0.5 accept JSON.
Legacy DiD/minimal-basis READMEs inspected: q1_partial_did_unobserved_trends p1_baseline/p1_baseline_v2 and q1_minimal_basis p1_staggered.
Partial-ID sensitivity descendants inspected: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec/v1 proposal, rejected angles, README, and reviewer JSONs with scalar-workflow strengthening paths.
Additional sensitivity bank mined: pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity/v1 failed proposal/reviews for quotient-certificate and rank-gap open problems.
Exact-ID analogue mined sparingly: eid_proximal_phase/v1 README/proposal/reviews for the failed scalar singular-value phase-transition pattern.
No web access used in Track A; sources were restricted to ThmSmith/doc/formalization/**.
